sections.paragraph:
ἐγκάρδια, ίων, τά from the adj. ἐγκάρδιος, ον ‘found in the heart’ (Democr.+; Philo, Spec. Leg. 1, 6) what is in the heart 2 Cl 9:9 (cod. H; cp. Philod., παρρ. Fgm. 28, 6 p. 14 Ol. τἀγκ. τις ἐρεῖ; Syntipas p. 10, 1). But the correct rdg. is perh. τὰ ἐν καρδίᾳ (2 Ch 32:31; Dt 8:2; 1 Km 9:19 al.).—S. DELG s.v. καρδία.

adj. adj. = adjective

Democr Democr , phil., V/IV b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philod Philod , I b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Syntipas Syntipas , a collection of fables under the name of Syntapis; before XI a.d.—List 5

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

perh. perh. = perhaps

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 273
